one_line_ask: License oversized beverage/lifestyle stock photos, reframe them DOWN to Fizzwild's desktop-hero / mobile-hero / section-header / CTA-card / promo-banner slots at exact pixel sizes, grade them into one cohesive vibrant family, vectorize and cut out the supplied wordmark, and deliver a web-ready pack plus a style reference so future assets stay consistent.
brief: Fizzwild Kombucha, a fictional small-batch craft kombucha brand relaunching its website, needs a cohesive set of website hero and banner graphics built entirely from licensed stock — no from-scratch illustration, no canvas expansion. The client has handed off a brand kit: a flat wordmark PNG (fizzwild_wordmark.png), a hex palette (brand teal #0E8C7F, sunburst amber #F5A623, off-white cream #FAF3E0), and a short text list of the section headers and promo copy that the dev team will typeset live (the freelancer lays in imagery + prepares the reusable wordmark, not body copy). The ask, quoting the brief, is "matching website graphics—hero images, section headers, and call-to-action spots—plus occasional banner ads for promotional campaigns," and every asset "should feel part of one cohesive family while being perfectly sized, compressed, and formatted for its destination." Deliverables and exact dimensions: (1) a desktop homepage hero at 1920x1080; (2) a mobile homepage hero at 1080x1920 built from a SECOND licensed shot (portrait crop) so the phone view is not just a squeezed desktop image; (3) an "Our Flavors" section-header strip at 1600x600; (4) a call-to-action card at 1080x1350;
